Samples were fixed using adhesive tape to the locations
at angles 0, 30, 60, 90, 120, and 150 deg with respect
to the d+ beam direction and TiT target center so that
simultaneous irradiations could be fulfilled in the
range of 13.4 to 14.8 MeV, and the distance from the
titanium-tritide target to the sample was 20 cm.
The RbCl sample, diameter 2.0 cm and thickness 1 mm,

The cerium sample was machined to a diameter of 2.0 cm
and thickness of 2 mm because of its friability.
Other samples had diameters of 2.0 to 2.4 cm,
thickness of 0.2 to 0.5 mm, with purity of 99.99%.
Details given in Table I in J.NSE,169,(2),191,2011.
The irradiation time was from 10 to 30 h,
depending on the half-life of the products.
